sutta: monks there are these 5 disadvantages of devotion to 1 monk what 5 monks when a person becomes very devoted to a monk and that monk falls into an error such that the sangha suspend him then he will think the sangha has suspended him who is dear and lovely to me and he will be no more full of devotion for the monks and from being without that devotion he will not follow other monks and from not following other monks he will not hear saddam true dhamma and from not hearing sat dhamma he will fall away from sat dhamma this monks is the first disadvantage of devotion to 1 monk that mang falls into an error such that the sangha make him sit on the outskirts of a sangha gathering and the person similarly loses devotion to the sangha or when a person becomes very devoted to among and that monk has gone to a distant place and he loses devotion to the sangha all the monk wanders in mind means becomes mentally disturbed all the monk is dead then he will think he is dead who was dear and lovely to me and he will not follow other monks and from not following other monks he will not hear the true dhamma and from not hearing the true dhamma he will fall away from the true dhamma this monks is the fifth disadvantage of devotion to 1 monk verily monks these are the 5 disadvantages of devotion to 1 monk as the end of the sutta

sutta: monks there are these 5 forms of stinginess what 5 stinginess as to 1's lodging stinginess as to 1's family stinginess as to gains stinginess as to fame and stinginess as to dhamma verily monks these are the 5 now the meanest of these 5 monks is this stinginess as to dhamma that's the end of the sutta
commentary: yeah there are certain things that we are stingy about and because we are attached to it once lodging family gains fame and the last 1 is dharma the meanest is the stinginess of dhamma and this is because the dhamma is of the greatest value of all those things mentioned the greatest value is the dhamma but even though the buddha said no no 1 should not be stingy concerning dhamma yet in the vinaya the buddha said if people who come to learn the dhamma they do not show enough respect for the dhamma then the monks are not allowed to teach them the dhamma for example they come and ask dharma they sit higher than a monk or they wear the head or they they don't take off the shoe or they are holding a weapon or something then the monk is not allowed to teach the dhamma to such people and we can see also during the buddha's time sometimes people came to the buddha and they did not ask for a dhamma teaching and so sometimes the buddha also would not teach them and sometimes they the ass and the buddha would just answer exactly only to the point what they asked and did not speak further so normally a per that the person that has to be engaged whether that person has the interest to listen to the dharma or not
